summary: The patient presents with signs indicative of vitamin K deficiency such as hematoma formation due to impaired coagulation factor function leading to prolonged INR times. This condition must be addressed through appropriate diagnosis and treatment targeting corrected levels of essential vitamins crucial for normal physiological processes including bone health and preventing excessive bleeding episodes seen here across multiple systems affected simultaneously. Key focus remains on identifying underlying cause related directly back to insufficient dietary intake vs other potential etiologies ruled out during evaluation process conducted thus far based upon available data reviewed thoroughly now compared previously when making original determination.
